_description: PIN-1: Payer-by-payer eligibility lookup requirements. Each payer accepts specific combinations of patient info for eligibility verification. The system checks OCR-extracted fields against these combos and flags exactly what's missing.
_usage: After M1B extracts patient fields and M1C identifies payer, check if any combo for that payer is fully satisfied by available data. If not, flag ALERT_INCOMPLETE_INSURANCE with the specific missing fields and the easiest combo to complete.

_global_notes.medicaid_id_fallback: For ALL Medicaids: if Medicaid ID unknown, look up in NV Medicaid FFS portal with other identifiers, then use retrieved ID for correct payer portal.
_global_notes.medicare_replacement_mbi: For ALL Medicare Advantage plans: MBI can be used in place of Advantage/Plan ID and will pull eligibility.
_global_notes.ssn_handling: SSN should never be stored in system. If SSN is the only option, flag for manual lookup by staff.

id_format_validation.10_digits: VALID — standard Aetna commercial ID. May or may not have 'W' prefix (W counts as part of the ID, not a separate prefix).
id_format_validation.W_plus_9_digits: VALID — same as 10-digit, the W is part of the ID.
id_format_validation.12_digits: ⚠️ AMBIGUOUS — could be Aetna Medicare (ACCEPTED) or Aetna HMO/Intermountain (OON — do NOT accept). See disambiguation rules below.

twelve_digit_disambiguation.description: 12-digit Aetna IDs require disambiguation between Medicare Advantage (accepted) and Intermountain HMO (OON).
clues_favoring_medicare: Referral from a Medicare-typical provider or geriatric office
Clinical notes mention Medicare, dual eligible, or age-related conditions
twelve_digit_disambiguation.action_if_uncertain: Process the entire referral as if Medicare (ACCEPTED) but add FLAG_VERIFY_AETNA_12DIGIT: 'Confirm this is Aetna Medicare, not Aetna HMO/Intermountain (OON). 12-digit ID is ambiguous.' Staff verifies during benefits call before scheduling.
twelve_digit_disambiguation.if_confirmed_oon: STOP_OON — Intermountain HMO is in the doNotAccept list.

Aetna.notes: Standard IDs are 10 digits (with or without W prefix). 12-digit IDs are ambiguous — could be Medicare (accepted) or Intermountain HMO (OON). Always flag 12-digit for verification.

Aetna Supplement.notes: Supplement IDs always start with 3 letters (often CLI) followed by numbers. Requires policy_number — not just member ID.

id_format_validation.format: Always starts with 3 letters followed by numbers
id_format_validation.common_prefix: CLI is the most common 3-letter prefix, but other letter combos exist
id_format_validation.if_no_letter_prefix: FLAG_VERIFY_AETNA_SUPPLEMENT_ID: ID does not start with 3 letters. May not be a supplement plan or may be OCR misread.
id_format_validation.note: The 3-letter prefix is part of the policy number, not a BCBS-style prefix.

id_format_validation.format: Almost always follows the 3-letter BCBS prefix format
id_format_validation.prefix_lookup: First 3 characters of ID → look up in bcbs_prefix_database.json to identify affiliate, plan type, and auth routing
id_format_validation.note: Anthem IS a BCBS affiliate. The prefix database is the primary tool for Anthem ID validation and routing.

Anthem.notes: Most flexible — patient_id + almost anything works. No name+DOB-only combo. IDs almost always follow BCBS 3-letter prefix format — use bcbs_prefix_database.json for identification and routing.

id_format_validation.format: Always starts with the letter H
id_format_validation.if_no_h_prefix: FLAG_VERIFY_HUMANA_ID: ID does not start with H. May be a different payer's ID, a Medicaid ID, or OCR misread. Verify with patient.
id_format_validation.medicaid_line: Humana Medicaid uses Medicaid ID (11 digits, all numeric) — NOT the H-prefixed Humana ID.

Humana.notes: Humana IDs always start with H. If ID doesn't start with H, flag for verification. Medicaid line uses Medicaid ID + DOB + State instead.

id_format_validation.format: 11 characters, mix of letters and numbers
id_format_validation.last_4: Always 2 letters followed by 2 numbers (e.g., AB12)
id_format_validation.first_7: Never more than 2 letters or 2 numbers in a row. If 3+ letters or 3+ numbers appear consecutively, the ID is INVALID.
id_format_validation.example_invalid_3_numbers: 123BC4DE567 — three numbers in a row (123) = invalid
id_format_validation.example_invalid_3_letters: 1ABC34DE567 — three letters in a row (ABC) = invalid
id_format_validation.if_invalid: FLAG_INVALID_MBI: ID does not match MBI format. May be an old HICN, a Medicare Advantage plan ID, or a data entry error. Verify with patient.

Medicare FFS.notes: MBI required. 11 chars, alternating pattern with no 3+ consecutive letters or numbers. Last 4 = 2 letters + 2 numbers. If MBI unknown, can look up with last+first+DOB+SSN.

id_format_validation.pattern_legacy: Typically starts with several leading zeros (e.g., 00012345678)
id_format_validation.pattern_newer: Some newer IDs have a few numbers, then zeros, then more numbers (e.g., 12300045678)
id_format_validation.always: Always exactly 11 digits. Always all numeric. If letters present, it is NOT a Medicaid ID.
id_format_validation.if_invalid: FLAG_INVALID_MEDICAID_ID: ID contains letters or is not 11 digits. May be a different payer's ID placed in wrong field.

Nevada Medicaid FFS.notes: Always 11 digits, all numeric. Legacy IDs start with leading zeros, newer ones may have zeros in the middle. Can look up in NV Medicaid portal using name+DOB if ID unknown.

Cigna.notes: IDs are sometimes all numbers, sometimes start with U. Many subsidiaries with varying formats. SSN accepted as alternative to Patient ID.

id_format_validation.format_variant_2: Starts with single U followed by numbers
id_format_validation.subsidiaries: Cigna has many random subsidiaries — ID format may vary. If unsure, process and flag for verification.
id_format_validation.note: No single reliable format. Accept and verify.

id_format_validation.format: Same as Cigna commercial — all numbers or U-prefix
id_format_validation.note: Supplement plan — verify Medicare is primary.

UMR.notes: Very limited — must have ID (or SSN) + DOB. No name-only lookup.

Sierra Health.notes: IDs are 11 digits, usually start with 1 or 2, often end 00/01/02. Very flexible lookup — patient ID alone works. SMA MRN = internal facility record number.

id_format_validation.format: 11 numerical digits. Usually starts with 1 or 2. Often ends with 00, 01, or 02.
id_format_validation.note: Same format across Sierra Health, HPN Commercial, and HPN Medicaid.

HPN.notes: IDs are 11 digits, usually start with 1 or 2, often end 00/01/02. Same portal as Sierra Health.

id_format_validation.format: 11 numerical digits. Usually starts with 1 or 2. Often ends with 00, 01, or 02.
id_format_validation.note: Same format across Sierra Health, HPN Commercial, and HPN Medicaid.

HPN Medicaid.notes: Accepts HPN plan ID (starts 1/2, ends 00/01/02) OR Medicaid ID (leading zeros). Also accepts name+DOB, SSN, or SMA MRN.

id_format_validation.medicaid_id_format: Medicaid ID format: always 11 digits, all numeric. Legacy IDs have leading zeros, newer ones may have zeros in the middle.
id_format_validation.hpn_plan_id_format: 11 numerical digits. Usually starts with 1 or 2. Often ends with 00, 01, or 02. Same format as commercial HPN/Sierra.
id_format_validation.note: Accepts EITHER the HPN plan ID (11 digits, starts 1/2) OR the Medicaid ID (11 digits, leading zeros). Both work.

Silversummit.notes: Covers Medicaid and AmBetter. Medicaid: 11-digit numeric. Ambetter: usually 2 letters (often UZ) + numbers, older plans may be 1 letter + numbers. Last name + DOB works without ID.

id_format_validation.medicaid_id_format: Medicaid ID format: always 11 digits, all numeric. Legacy IDs have leading zeros, newer ones may have zeros in the middle.
id_format_validation.ambetter_format: Ambetter IDs usually start with 2 letters followed by numbers (often UZ, but not limited to UZ). Older plans may have single letter prefix followed by numbers.
id_format_validation.note: Silversummit covers both Medicaid and Ambetter lines. Check which line based on ID format.

Tricare.id_note: ⚠️ The DBN is typically found in the Member ID field on referral documents. If OCR extracts a member_id for a Tricare patient, treat it as patient_dbn — it satisfies the DBN combo.

id_format_validation.10_digits: ⚠️ INVALID — this is the DOD ID, which does NOT work for eligibility verification. Flag for staff to obtain correct 9-digit SSN or 11-digit DBN.

Tricare.notes: ⚠️ Cannot verify with Member ID alone — but what's labeled 'Member ID' on referrals is usually the DBN, which DOES work. Need DBN (11 digits) or SSN (9 digits). If 10 digits found, that's the DOD ID — flag as invalid. Tricare For Life must CALL to verify — portal won't work.

Caresource.notes: Medicaid plan. Can use Medicaid ID if plan ID unknown.

id_format_validation.medicaid_id_format: Medicaid ID format: always 11 digits, all numeric. Legacy IDs have leading zeros, newer ones may have zeros in the middle.

Molina.notes: ⚠️ State is REQUIRED in all combos. Nevada = 'NV'.

id_format_validation.medicaid_id_format: Medicaid ID format: always 11 digits, all numeric. Legacy IDs have leading zeros, newer ones may have zeros in the middle.
id_format_validation.note: State is REQUIRED in all combos.

UHC.notes: Commercial: 9 digits starting with 9. Surest: 12 digits starting with 771/777. Medicare: 9-11 digits.

id_format_validation.commercial: Almost always 9 digits starting with 9. Sometimes starts with other numbers but 9 is most common.
id_format_validation.surest: Always 12 digits. Almost always starts with 771 or 777. If 12-digit ID starting with 771/777 on a UHC card → Surest plan.
id_format_validation.note: All UHC ID types are numeric. If letters present (other than MBI format), may be misidentified payer.

AARP UHC Supplement.notes: Patient ID required for all combos. No name+DOB-only option.

id_format_validation.format: Numeric, follows UHC Medicare format (9-11 digits)
id_format_validation.note: Supplement plan — verify Medicare is primary.

BCBS.notes: Generic BCBS. Specific affiliates may have additional or different combos. Prefix determines affiliate routing.

id_format_validation.format: Always starts with G followed by numbers
id_format_validation.if_no_g_prefix: FLAG_VERIFY_GEHA_ID: ID does not start with G. May not be GEHA or may be OCR misread.

GEHA.notes: GEHA IDs always start with G followed by numbers.

id_format_validation.format: UHSS plan IDs always start with 800 followed by numbers
id_format_validation.if_no_800_prefix: FLAG_VERIFY_LVFF_ID: ID does not start with 800. May not be the correct UHSS plan.

LV Firefighters.notes: LV Firefighters UHSS plan. IDs always start with 800 followed by numbers.

field_definitions.member_id: Alias for patient_id (some payers use this term)
field_definitions.policy_number: Policy/plan number (distinct from member ID, used by some supplements)
field_definitions.ssn: Social Security Number — ⚠️ flag for manual staff lookup, never store
field_definitions.state: State of residence (required by some Medicaid plans)
field_definitions.sma_mrn: Sleep Medicine Associates internal medical record number
field_definitions.sponsor_ssn: Tricare sponsor's SSN (service member, not necessarily patient)
field_definitions.patient_dbn: Tricare Defense Benefits Number (alternative to SSN)

ocr_field_mapping._description: Maps OCR-extracted fields to eligibility field names
patient.name: last_name
first_name
